# Awesome List Submissions

TrustLayer should be submitted to the following awesome lists for better discoverability:

## Lists to Submit To

### 1. awesome-llm-tools
- **Repo:** https://github.com/nglomerocks/awesome-llm-tools
- **Category:** Suggested: "Verification & Trust" or "Multi-Provider Tools"
- **Description:** Universal trust layer for AI tools with verification engine, cost tracking, and model comparison

### 2. awesome-local-ai
- **Repo:** https://github.com/janhq/awesome-local-ai
- **Category:** Suggested: "Applications" or "Tools"
- **Description:** Local-first AI verification and trust layer. Works offline with Ollama, supports Claude/OpenAI/Gemini

### 3. awesome-ai-safety
- **Repo:** https://github.com/gohyi/awesome-ai-safety
- **Category:** Suggested: "Safety Tools" or "Verification"
- **Description:** Verification engine and trust scoring system for AI outputs. Detects hallucinations and overconfidence.

## Submission Template

When submitting, use this format:

```markdown
- [TrustLayer](https://github.com/acunningham-ship-it/trustlayer) - Universal trust layer for every AI tool. Verify outputs, track costs, compare models, keep data local. Features: verification engine with 0-100 trust scoring, multi-provider comparison, cost tracking, offline knowledge base, local-first design.
```

## Next Steps

1. Fork each awesome list repo
2. Add TrustLayer entry to appropriate section
3. Create pull request with clear description
4. Reference the TrustLayer repo in the PR
5. Once merged, add the awesome badge to README
